annotations.label: Expected value (informal)

anchor.snippet: is a generalization of the weighted average

annotations.note: Mathlib's Bochner integral `MeasureTheory.integral` is the measure-theoretic generalization of the weighted average that defines expectation against any (probability) measure.

annotations.label: Expected value of a random vector/matrix

anchor.snippet: Any definition of expected value may be extended to define an expected value of a multidimensional random variable

annotations.note: The Bochner integral is defined for any function valued in a normed space, so vector- and matrix-valued expectations are subsumed by the same `MeasureTheory.integral`.

annotations.label: Expectation for finitely many outcomes

anchor.section: Random variables with finitely many outcomes

annotations.note: Integration with respect to a finitely-supported measure (e.g. a finite sum of weighted Diracs) reduces the Bochner integral to the finite weighted sum form, captured by `integral_finset`/`integral_const`.

annotations.label: Fair six-sided die

anchor.snippet: represent the outcome of a roll of a fair six-sided die

annotations.note: No grep hit for a worked die-roll expectation example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Roulette $1 straight-up bet

anchor.snippet: The roulette game consists of a small ball and a wheel with 38 numbered pockets around the edge

annotations.note: No grep hit for a roulette expected-value example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Expectation for countably infinite outcomes

anchor.section: Random variables with countably infinitely many outcomes
anchor.snippet: Informally, the expectation of a random variable with a countably infinite set of possible outcomes is defined analogously

annotations.note: Countable sums of weighted point-mass expectations are captured by Bochner integration plus `integral_tsum`/`lintegral_tsum` against a countably supported measure.

annotations.label: Logarithmic-series distribution expectation

anchor.snippet: is the scaling factor which makes the probabilities sum to 1

annotations.note: No grep hit for a logarithmic-series distribution in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Expectation via probability density function

anchor.snippet: which has a probability density function given by a function

annotations.note: `integral_pdf_smul` expresses `∫ f x · pdf X x dx = ∫ f ∘ X dℙ`, the density-based formula for expectation of `f(X)`.

annotations.label: Expectation via Lebesgue integral

anchor.snippet: All definitions of the expected value may be expressed in the language of measure theory

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `MeasureTheory.integral` as the Bochner integral (with `lintegral` for the nonnegative Lebesgue case), which is the measure-theoretic expectation.

annotations.label: Absolutely continuous random variable

anchor.snippet: is said to be absolutely continuous if any of the following conditions are satisfied

annotations.note: `HasPDF X ℙ μ` (with `HasPDF.absolutelyContinuous : map X ℙ ≪ μ`) is Mathlib's notion of a random variable with a density / absolutely continuous law.

annotations.label: Expectation via CDF area equality

anchor.snippet: can also be defined on the graph of its cumulative distribution function

annotations.note: Mathlib has `ProbabilityTheory.cdf` and Stieltjes integration infrastructure, but no single lemma equating `E[X]` to the signed area between the CDF and the axes was found.

annotations.label: Daily precipitation model
annotations.note: No grep hit for a precipitation/mixed-distribution worked example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinite expected values (positive/negative parts)

anchor.snippet: it is fundamental to be able to consider expected values

annotations.note: This lemma decomposes a real Bochner integral as the difference of the Lebesgue integrals of the positive and negative parts, the standard formal definition allowing infinite expectations.

annotations.label: St. Petersburg paradox expectation

anchor.snippet: In the case of the St. Petersburg paradox

annotations.note: No grep hit for the St. Petersburg paradox in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tail-sum formula

anchor.snippet: the expected value can also be expressed in terms of its tail probabilities

annotations.note: Mathlib's layercake formulas (`Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Layercake`) generalize the tail-sum identity `E[X] = ∫₀^∞ ℙ(X > t) dt` for nonnegative random variables.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of expectation
annotations.note: `integral_nonneg_of_ae` states `0 ≤ᵐ[μ] f → 0 ≤ ∫ f ∂μ`.

annotations.label: Linearity of expectation
annotations.note: `integral_add` together with `integral_smul` give linearity of the Bochner integral / expectation.

annotations.label: Monotonicity of expectation
annotations.note: `integral_mono` provides `f ≤ g → ∫ f ≤ ∫ g` under integrability assumptions.

annotations.label: Non-degeneracy of expectation
annotations.note: `integral_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg_ae` characterises when the integral of a nonneg integrable function vanishes (iff `f =ᵐ 0`).

annotations.label: Almost-sure equality preserves expectation

anchor.snippet: if X and Y are random variables that take different values with probability zero

annotations.note: `integral_congr_ae` shows `f =ᵐ[μ] g → ∫ f ∂μ = ∫ g ∂μ`.

annotations.label: Expectation of an almost-sure constant
annotations.note: `integral_const : ∫ _, c ∂μ = μ.real univ • c` gives `E[c] = c` under a probability measure.

annotations.label: Absolute value bound on expectation
annotations.note: `norm_integral_le_integral_norm` is the normed-space form of `|E[X]| ≤ E[|X|]`.

annotations.label: Expectation of indicator equals probability

anchor.snippet: denote the indicator function of an event A

annotations.note: `integral_indicator_one` proves `∫ 𝟙_A dμ = μ.real A`, i.e. `E[𝟙_A] = ℙ(A)`.

annotations.label: Expectation formulas in terms of CDF
annotations.note: Mathlib has `ProbabilityTheory.cdf` and Stieltjes integration, but no specific identity expressing `E[X]` directly as an integral against the CDF was located.

annotations.label: Non-multiplicativity and independence
annotations.note: `IndepFun.integral_mul_eq_mul_integral` gives `E[XY] = E[X]·E[Y]` for independent integrable real random variables.

annotations.label: Law of the unconscious statistician
annotations.note: `integral_map` proves `∫ f d(map φ μ) = ∫ f ∘ φ dμ`, i.e. `E[g(X)] = ∫ g d(law of X)`.

annotations.label: Markov's inequality

anchor.snippet: Markov's inequality is among the best-known and simplest to prove

annotations.note: `mul_meas_ge_le_lintegral` is the Markov inequality `ε·μ{f ≥ ε} ≤ ∫⁻ f dμ`; the dedicated file `ChebyshevMarkov.lean` also packages variants.

annotations.label: Chebyshev's inequality
annotations.note: `meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq` is exactly Chebyshev's inequality `ℙ(|X − E[X]| ≥ c) ≤ Var(X)/c²`.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov inequality

anchor.snippet: The Kolmogorov inequality extends the Chebyshev inequality

annotations.note: Mathlib has Doob's submartingale maximal inequality (`maximal_ineq`), which subsumes the partial-sum Kolmogorov inequality, but no statement specialized to independent zero-mean partial sums was found.

annotations.label: Jensen's inequality
annotations.note: `ConvexOn.map_integral_le` proves `g(∫ f dμ) ≤ ∫ g ∘ f dμ` for convex `g` under a probability measure — Jensen's inequality.

annotations.label: Lyapunov inequality
annotations.note: `eLpNorm_le_eLpNorm_of_exponent_le` is exactly Lyapunov's inequality: `‖X‖_p ≤ ‖X‖_q` for `p ≤ q` under a probability measure.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality
annotations.note: `ENNReal.lintegral_mul_le_Lp_mul_Lq` is Hölder's inequality for integrals; analogous Bochner/Lp statements are also in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality
annotations.note: The `p = q = 2` case of `lintegral_mul_le_Lp_mul_Lq`, together with `L2Space.lean` lemmas, gives the integral Cauchy–Schwarz inequality.

annotations.label: Minkowski inequality
annotations.note: `eLpNorm_add_le` is the Minkowski (Lp triangle) inequality.

annotations.label: Monotone convergence theorem

anchor.section: Expectations under convergence of random variables

annotations.note: `lintegral_iSup` is the monotone convergence theorem for nonnegative measurable functions.

annotations.label: Fatou's lemma

anchor.section: Expectations under convergence of random variables

annotations.note: `lintegral_liminf_le` is Fatou's lemma: `∫⁻ liminf fₙ ≤ liminf ∫⁻ fₙ`.

annotations.label: Dominated convergence theorem

anchor.section: Expectations under convergence of random variables

annotations.note: `tendsto_integral_of_dominated_convergence` is Lebesgue's dominated convergence theorem for Bochner integrals.

annotations.label: Uniform integrability and convergence of expectations

anchor.section: Expectations under convergence of random variables

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `UniformIntegrable` and several consequences (e.g. `UniformIntegrable.memLp_of_tendstoInMeasure`), but a direct `E[Xₙ] → E[X]` style statement matching the Wikipedia wording was not found.

annotations.label: Density via characteristic function inversion

anchor.snippet: is related to its characteristic function

annotations.note: No characteristic-function inversion formula recovering the density was found in Mathlib's `CharacteristicFunction` files.

annotations.label: Expectation via characteristic function (Plancherel)

anchor.snippet: we can use this inversion formula to obtain

annotations.note: `charFun_eq_integral_innerProbChar` defines `charFun` as `E[e^{i⟨X,t⟩}]`, but a Plancherel-style identity expressing arbitrary `E[g(X)]` via the characteristic function is not present.
